pattern: Every finite-or-repeating decimal is exactly a fraction, so parse each string into a numerator/denominator and compare the reduced fractions instead of floating-point values.
hints: Floating point cannot decide equality here; convert each string to an exact rational and compare those.
A repeating block (d) over m repeating digits contributes a fraction of the block over (10^m - 1), shifted by the non-repeating decimal places.
Split into integer, non-repeating, and repeating parts. Value = integer + nonrep/10^a + rep/((10^b - 1) * 10^a). Use exact integer arithmetic (e.g. fractions) and test for equality.

edge_inputs.description: two different repeating notations of the same value
different placement of the repeating block
nines repeating equals the next integer
non-repeating decimals that are equal with trailing zeros
repeating zero equals a finite decimal
integer part only on both sides, equal

stress.description: random rational strings, sometimes equal by construction, in varied notations
stress.note: half the cases are exactly equal strings to test the true branch
